req.title: Open savings account (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path account creation for a verified customer with initial deposit >= $100, including tier assignment; (2) rejection of deposits below $100 (422); (3) the exact boundary value of $100 (minimum allowed); (4) idempotency guarantee on duplicate submission. Four tests are generated: positive/EP for a well-above-minimum deposit, negative/NEG for below-minimum, boundary/BVA for exactly $100, and positive/STC for the idempotency state transition (first call creates, second call returns identical result). Each test uses a per-test unique Idempotency-Key derived from uuid4 to ensure determinism and test-level isolation.

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit
preconditions: customer auth token available via <verified_customer_token> fixture
actions: POST /accounts/savings with initial_balance=500.00 and Authorization header
Assert HTTP 201 and response body fields
trace: R-FIN-077: verified customer opens savings account
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit(api_client, verified_customer_token):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }
    payload = {"initial_balance": 500.00}

    response = api_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "account_id" in body, "Missing account_id in response"
    assert body.get("interest_tier") is not None, "interest_tier must be assigned"
    assert float(body.get("balance", 0)) == 500.00, "Balance mismatch"

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_rejected
preconditions: customer auth token available via <verified_customer_token> fixture
actions: POST /accounts/savings with initial_balance=99.99 and Authorization header
expected: response body contains error field referencing minimum balance constraint
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_rejected(api_client, verified_customer_token):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }
    payload = {"initial_balance": 99.99}

    response = api_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert any(k in error_text for k in ("minimum", "balance", "initial")), \
        f"Error body should reference minimum balance constraint: {body}"

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_at_exact_minimum_boundary
preconditions: customer auth token available via <verified_customer_token> fixture
actions: POST /accounts/savings with initial_balance=100.00 and Authorization header
trace: R-FIN-077: tier assigned automatically at boundary
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_at_exact_minimum_boundary(api_client, verified_customer_token):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }
    payload = {"initial_balance": 100.00}

    response = api_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at boundary $100, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "account_id" in body, "account_id must be present"
    assert float(body.get("balance", -1)) == 100.00, "Balance must equal 100.00"
    assert body.get("interest_tier") is not None, "Tier must be assigned at boundary"

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_idempotent_duplicate_submission
preconditions: customer auth token available via <verified_customer_token> fixture
same Idempotency-Key reused for both requests (state: created -> re-submitted)
actions: Generate one Idempotency-Key shared by both calls
POST /accounts/savings (first submission) - account transitions from non-existent to created
POST /accounts/savings (second submission with same key) - state remains created
trace: R-FIN-077: idempotency - duplicate submission returns original result
R-FIN-077: state transition coverage - created state returned unchanged on replay
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_idempotent_duplicate_submission(api_client, verified_customer_token):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key,
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
    }
    payload = {"initial_balance": 250.00}

    first = api_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201, f"First call expected 201, got {first.status_code}"
    first_body = first.json()

    second = api_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code == 201, f"Second call expected 201, got {second.status_code}"
    second_body = second.json()

    assert first_body["account_id"] == second_body["account_id"], "account_id must match"
    assert first_body["interest_tier"] == second_body["interest_tier"], "tier must match"
    assert float(first_body["balance"]) == float(second_body["balance"]), "balance must match"
